title: Level 191
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 191: "Facades" }} {{Fix|date=June 25, 2026|1=unusual paragraph spacing, how is it a motel if the images are from a mall? Motels are never this expansive.}} {{Class Custom Image|bg-image=Motel 1.png|bg-size=750px|text-color=rgb(210,220,210)|class=3|entity=Entity Infestation|hexagon=3|safety=Unsafe}}{{Blurbackground|file=Motel 1.png|opacity=50}} Level 191 is the 192nd Level of the Backrooms. == Description == {{Blurimage|file=Motel 1.png|caption=A dark lounge.}}Level 191, also known as "Facades", is an unusual level resembling an extremely confusingly structured motel. The unintuitive layout of the environment gives the impression of a building that was initially meant for a different use and was lazily renovated, or the blueprints of which were drawn up by a class of blindfolded kindergarteners. Exterior facades on the interior, windows and doorways leading into solid walls, and an unnessecary amount of 45-degree angles combine to make Level 191 one of the most architecturally confusing levels ever discovered. Brick pillars, plaster walls, wood accents, tiled & carpeted floors, and a drop ceiling compose most of the levels' structure in varying percentages depending on one's position. If a wanderer enters this Level with the expectation that its layout conforms to any known logic, they will be sorely disappointed. One might assume that the variety in Level 191's environment would make it easier to navigate. In actuality, however, each room may have anywhere from 5-10 doorways leading into different hallways, lounges or atriums, making it near impossible to explore every avenue they may come across. Every new room is a transitional space, which means there are no amenities or resources to be found. {{Quote|It's as though the inside and outside have been reversed... where's the bedrooms, the closets, the bathrooms? I almost prefer the industrialism of Level 1 over this. At least then I knew what to expect, instead of walking through lounge after desolate lounge...|Unknown|color=White}}{{Blurimage|file=Motel 3.png|caption=An atrium.}}The level is illuminated by a wide variety of artificial light sources; in most areas, either rectangular fluorescents or smaller circular lights set into the white drop ceiling. However, some parts may also include metallic, green-hued wall or floor mounted lamps, along with cylindrical yellow wall lamps. Luckily, this means that entirely pitch black areas of Level 191 are extremely infrequent, so wanderers should not need to bring their own light sources when traveling here. Some rooms even contain functional light switches (equally likely to be adorned to the floor, ceiling or walls), though which set of lights they turn off seems completely random. In an area with four different types of lights, one switch was reported to toggle exactly 3 lamp posts, 12 ceiling lights and 7 wall lamps, with all other light sources in the area remaining lit no matter how many times the switch was flipped. Additionally, Level 191 contains one of the highest densities of native plant life for an "indoors" level. Many arrangements of controlled bushes, potted plants and even small trees are scattered around. The leaves of said plants are known to be edible, since they are not synthetic or plastic, and may be consumed in lieu of conventional food items should the need arise (or if one is trapped in a confined space within Level 191 for a prolonged period of time, such as when cornered by an entity). {{Blurimage|file=Motel 2.png|caption=Interior facades.}} Like the rest of the environment, the staircases in Level 191 are also strangely constructed and largely unhelpful. They are frequently located in the lounges or atriums, and often only provide passage between two floors, despite the Level being theorized to extended infinitely in every direction. This makes vertical exploration of Level 191 much more difficult than traveling horizontally, though its structure does not seem to vary outside of its typical strangeness and distortions based on how deep wanderers have traveled. The overall temperature in Level 191 remains at a cool and consistent 60 degrees Fahrenheit, approximately 10 degrees less than the low end of the room temperature spectrum. The atmosphere is maintained by a variety of air conditioners throughout the level running at maximum efficiency, the noise of which creates a quiet cacophony of chilling machinery. There are no thermostats present, either; if one were to wish to change the temperature in any way, they would have to dismantle the nearby AC systems, which would be both time-consuming and noisy, potentially alerting nearby entities. Though not inherently dangerous, this aspect of Level 191 adds to the general discomfort of the environment. == Entities == Level 191 appears to be infested with [[Entity 5-A|Amalgamations]]. Almost every wanderer who has traversed it has also reported seeing, hearing or being chased by one through its perilous halls. Luckily, the unintuitive layout of Level 191 allows many opportunities for escape, should you find yourself being pursued. Ideally, one should avoid long hallways or open areas where the entity can build up speed, and turn as many corners as possible to get out of its line of sight. If neither of those strategies are an option, hiding or squeezing through a narrow space may also cause the entity to lose interest. Interestingly, the primary composition of the [[Entity 5-A|Amalgamations]] in Level 191 is the corpses of [[Entity 4|Deathmoths]], which would normally indicate an adjacent infestation; however, no living [[Entity 4|Deathmoths]] have yet been reported in Level 191. == Colonies & Outposts == As of yet, no colonies or outposts have been established or documented in Level 191. Though the level is constructed with semi-malleable materials, such as those used to make similar structures in the Frontrooms, attempting to modify it is not recommended. Aside from being inefficient, inexperienced architects may become exposed to loose wiring or accidentally bring down load-bearing walls (the latter of which killed three renovators during the only documented attempt to colonize Level 191). As such, erecting new constructs instead would seem the ideal choice, should such an attempt be repeated. == Entrances & Exits == === Entrances === * Progressing through a series of increasingly furnished ballrooms in [[Level 5]] will seamlessly transition one to this Level. * Larger rooms deep into [[Level 0]] may contain facades that lead here. === Exits === Due to the abundance of doors, doorways, windows and exit signs of various types already present in Level 191, locating an exit can be extremely difficult. One may pass several exits that appear to lead to more ordinary rooms before finding a more widely recognizable one. * One may encounter a window with [[Level 4]] visible on the other side. It can be unlatched and opened or forcibly broken to enter. * A supermarket facade clipped into an otherwise ordinary hallway will lead to [[Level 197]] (these may be difficult to discern from Level 191's other interior facades; look for appropriate signage). * A solid metal door labeled "fire exit" will take wanderers into [[Level 69]].
